role_title: Technical Program Manager,  Identity and Access Management Programs

level_signals: 5+ years of technical program management experience
Drives large-scale technical programs across multiple teams
Influences decisions across teams without direct authority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of technical program management experience with demonstrated success in security-focused initiatives
Experience managing complex technical programs with multiple stakeholders and dependencies
Strong understanding of security concepts and best practices, particularly in API security, authentication systems, and access controls
Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate complex technical concepts to various audiences
Proven track record of influencing decisions across teams without direct authority
Experience working with engineering and product teams in a fast-paced environment
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent practical experience)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with payment systems, financial technology, or enterprise SaaS platforms
Knowledge of compliance requirements related to data security (SOC2, PCI, etc.)
Experience with identity and access management systems
Background in API development or platform security
Previous experience working on developer platforms or developer experience

scope.surface: Organization Access Platform (OAP) — API key security, Account Takeover (ATO) prevention, login access controls, risk/security/fraud prevention
primary_metrics_inferred: Program effectiveness metrics and security outcomes
core_responsibilities: Lead end-to-end program management for critical security initiatives including API key security, Account Takeover (ATO) prevention, login access controls, and other risk/security/fraud prevention measures
Drive cross-team dependencies and collaboration between security, fraud operations, and product engineering teams
Track, communicate, and resolve program blockers across multiple workstreams
Develop implementation roadmaps and execution strategies for complex security features
Partner with engineering managers to define technical requirements and prioritize security enhancements
Establish metrics and reporting processes to measure program effectiveness and security outcomes
Facilitate decision-making processes when trade-offs are required between security, user experience, and development timelines
Represent OAP security initiatives in cross-functional meetings and leadership reviews

green_flags: Clearly scoped security domain — OAP team with specific focus areas named (ATO, API key security, login controls)
JD explicitly states preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement — inclusive application signal
Stripe is a well-known, high-scale financial infrastructure company with strong engineering culture
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy specified — candidates cannot assess work arrangement
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information provided

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)

location.type (not stated in JD body; set to 'unclear')
